name: Domaine du Rayol - Le Jardin des Méditerranées
summary: Mediterranean garden with landscapes from comparable climates worldwide, seasonal activities and a marine-garden/underwater-trail angle; just outside the core gulf but relevant as a premium half-day nature detour.
parking_notes: Official access/parking details still need practical check before publication.
best_time: Spring, early summer or autumn; avoid treating peak hot afternoons as default.

name: Fort-Freinet
summary: Fortified medieval site above La Garde-Freinet, accessible only on foot, with castrum remains and broad Massif des Maures context.
parking_notes: Official page mentions access from the village or Parking de la Croix; exact parking logistics to verify.
best_time: Clear morning or late afternoon; avoid wet or windy conditions because the official page warns of a steep path.

name: Pont des Fées
summary: Former water-supply aqueduct/heritage site below Grimaud, useful as a quieter heritage walk paired with the village rather than Port Grimaud crowds.
parking_notes: Parking and walking route from Grimaud village need local verification.
best_time: Morning or shoulder season, especially if pairing with Grimaud village.

name: Moulin de Paillas
summary: Restored windmill area above Ramatuelle on the exposed Paillas ridge, with strong viewpoint potential over village, vineyards and coast.
parking_notes: Road/parking and access limits need official confirmation.
best_time: Golden hour or clear mistral-after day; avoid overselling until access is checked.

name: Phare du Cap Camarat
summary: Lighthouse and protected coastal headland context near Cap Camarat; useful for a restrained viewpoint/nature note rather than a beach recommendation.
parking_notes: Access, parking and any restricted areas near lighthouse/semaphore need official confirmation.
best_time: Clear early morning or late afternoon; keep visitor guidance conservative because the cap is protected.

name: Chapelle Sainte-Anne
summary: Hilltop chapel above Saint-Tropez with maritime ex-voto history and cultural/film associations; generally not an always-open attraction.
parking_notes: Parking/access and exact public opening days need official confirmation.
best_time: Use as viewpoint/context stop, not as guaranteed interior visit.

name: Cimetière marin de Saint-Tropez
summary: Sea-edge cemetery below the Citadelle, relevant for a quiet reflective walk and Saint-Tropez cultural context when described respectfully.
parking_notes: Best approached on foot from the Citadelle/old town; access rules and opening hours need official confirmation.
best_time: Quiet morning; keep tone respectful and avoid celebrity-tour framing.

name: Château Minuty
summary: Major Saint-Tropez peninsula wine estate with official caveau visits, tastings and self-guided audioguide; strongest first wine stop candidate because current visit formats are documented by the estate.
parking_notes: Parking and exact arrival logistics still need direct visitor check before publication.
best_time: Avoid vendanges restrictions; official caveau hours and reservation rules must be checked again close to travel.

name: Château des Marres
summary: Family winery candidate mentioned in recent Saint-Tropez rosé editorial context; needs primary-source verification before any recommendation.
parking_notes: Parking, tasting access and address need primary-source confirmation.
best_time: Candidate for a quieter alternative to Minuty, but not yet verified.

name: Port Grimaud
summary: Canal town within Grimaud, created by François Spoerry from the 1960s and known as a Venise provençale-style stop with canals, bridges, church and waterside houses.
parking_notes: Visitor parking, access restrictions and current tariffs need official/local confirmation.
best_time: Morning or late afternoon for light and lower crowd pressure; avoid reducing it to only an Instagram stop.

name: Saint-Tropez old town
summary: Historic fishing-port and resort town with port, old streets, Citadelle/cultural context, restaurants and nearby beaches; needs practical timing to avoid peak congestion.
parking_notes: Use official municipal parking source; old-core access should be planned rather than improvised.
best_time: Morning for market/old town or late afternoon for port walk; avoid midday car approach in peak season.

name: Grimaud village
summary: Perched village above the gulf with castle context and historic lanes; useful contrast to Port Grimaud's waterside plan.
parking_notes: Village parking and walking route to Pont des Fées need official/local confirmation.
best_time: Morning or golden hour; pair with Port Grimaud or Pont des Fées.

name: Gassin village
summary: Hilltop village near Saint-Tropez, listed among Les Plus Beaux Villages de France in source material, with ramparts, viewpoints and narrow lanes.
parking_notes: Parking and viewpoint access need official/local confirmation.
best_time: Late afternoon for view and dinner; avoid overpromising restaurant value until checked.

name: Ramatuelle village
summary: Hill village above Pampelonne with medieval context, useful for combining village atmosphere, beaches, vineyards and Paillas/Camarat viewpoints.
parking_notes: Parking and pedestrian access need official/local confirmation.
best_time: Morning or early evening outside peak beach-transfer traffic.
